content.full_text:
/ 
Consolidated Candidate/Committee Report 
If two candidates control one committee, they may 
file in the following manner: 
1) 
each candidate may file a Form 430 and 
the committee may file one 420, or 
2) 
each candidate may file a 490 consolidated 
candidate/committee report. 
Two candidates and a controlled committee may 
not file a single 490.

sections.conclusion: If two candidates control one committee, they may file in the following manner: 1) each candidate may file a Form 430 and the committee may file one 420, or 2) each candidate may file a 490 consolidated candidate/committee report. Two candidates and a controlled committee may not file a single 490.
sections.question_synthetic: May two candidates who control a single committee file consolidated campaign finance reports, and if so, what are the permissible filing options?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Two candidates controlling one committee may file either individual Forms 430 with a single Form 420 for the committee, or each candidate may file a Form 490 consolidated report. However, all three entities cannot file a single consolidated Form 490.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document appears to be a brief advice excerpt without traditional question/conclusion section headers. No formal salutation, signature, or requestor identification present. Confidence reduced due to missing structural elements typical of formal FPPC advice letters.

QUESTION: May two candidates who control a single committee file consolidated campaign finance reports, and if so, what are the permissible filing options?

CONCLUSION: If two candidates control one committee, they may file in the following manner: 1) each candidate may file a Form 430 and the committee may file one 420, or 2) each candidate may file a 490 consolidated candidate/committee report. Two candidates and a controlled committee may not file a single 490.

Two candidates and a controlled committee may not file a single 490.
embedding.summary: FPPC advice on filing options for two candidates controlling a single campaign committee. The letter specifies permissible consolidated reporting formats under California campaign finance law.
